section_name: Contract Allowing Overweight Vehicle with Commodities or Products to Cross Highway; Surety Bond
chapter_title: Permits for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
text:
(a) A person may operate a vehicle that exceeds the overall gross weight limits provided by Section 621.101 (Maximum Weight of Vehicle or Combination) to cross the width of a highway from private property to other private property if:

(1) the vehicle is transporting grain, sand, or another commodity or product and the vehicle’s overall gross weight is not heavier than 110,000 pounds; or

(2) the vehicle is an unlicensed vehicle that is transporting sand, gravel, stones, rock, caliche, or a similar commodity.

(b) Before a person may operate a vehicle under this section, the person must:

(1) contract with the Texas Department of Transportation to indemnify the Texas Department of Transportation for the cost of the maintenance and repair for damage caused by a vehicle crossing that part of the highway; and

(2) execute an adequate surety bond to compensate for the cost of maintenance and repair, approved by the comptroller and the attorney general, with a corporate surety authorized to do business in this state, conditioned on the person fulfilling each obligation of the agreement.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 623. Permits for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
##### § 623.052 — Contract Allowing Overweight Vehicle with Commodities or Products to Cross Highway; Surety Bond

(a) A person may operate a vehicle that exceeds the overall gross weight limits provided by Section 621.101 (Maximum Weight of Vehicle or Combination) to cross the width of a highway from private property to other private property if:

    (1) the vehicle is transporting grain, sand, or another commodity or product and the vehicle’s overall gross weight is not heavier than 110,000 pounds; or

    (2) the vehicle is an unlicensed vehicle that is transporting sand, gravel, stones, rock, caliche, or a similar commodity.

(b) Before a person may operate a vehicle under this section, the person must:

    (1) contract with the Texas Department of Transportation to indemnify the Texas Department of Transportation for the cost of the maintenance and repair for damage caused by a vehicle crossing that part of the highway; and

    (2) execute an adequate surety bond to compensate for the cost of maintenance and repair, approved by the comptroller and the attorney general, with a corporate surety authorized to do business in this state, conditioned on the person fulfilling each obligation of the agreement.

*Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1345 (S.B.1420), Sec. 75, eff. September 1, 2011.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A person may operate a vehicle that exceeds the overall gross weight limits provided by Section 621.101 (Maximum Weight of Vehicle or Combination) to cross the width of a highway from private property to other private property if:
the vehicle is transporting grain, sand, or another commodity or product and the vehicle’s overall gross weight is not heavier than 110,000 pounds; or
the vehicle is an unlicensed vehicle that is transporting sand, gravel, stones, rock, caliche, or a similar commodity.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: Before a person may operate a vehicle under this section, the person must:

contract with the Texas Department of Transportation to indemnify the Texas Department of Transportation for the cost of the maintenance and repair for damage caused by a vehicle crossing that part of the highway; and

execute an adequate surety bond to compensate for the cost of maintenance and repair, approved by the comptroller and the attorney general, with a corporate surety authorized to do business in this state, conditioned on the person fulfilling each obligation of the agreement.
